summary: Systems designed and validated the controlled R1 authoritative client runtime task graph. Exactly seven DRAFT child tasks cover production Worker lifecycle, command/query/preview/submit authority, revisioned read models, authoritative time controls, Web/Electron save-load-restart and crash recovery, hermetic production-path evidence, and the R1 exit gate. The dependency graph is acyclic and conservative: before parent closure taskctl ready is empty, and after RR1-TASK-GRAPH-001 is CLOSED on origin/main only RR1-WEB-WORKER-RUNTIME-001 becomes dependency-ready. No product, package, test, content, CI, tool, dependency, roadmap, or historical Gate file was changed.

tests_run.result: Exactly the parent task modification and seven new RR1 child task files were present before handoff creation.

Kickoff commit resolved exactly to b8c91f0116feee5ba8a9a760d24e261c04d157f1; final validation continued atop successor-Lead delivery commit 24a93d9cc536b3563afce0a0344438308eeb7962, which includes handoff commit 8864075f450a1a459f5bd53c8bdd7d8a8a273ae3 and route-delivery commit 9fc58f66f8107e2a66190085046ac609818fe95f.
VALID; parent status REVIEW and route_to qa_reviewer.
VALID; DRAFT and dependency-gated by RR1-TASK-GRAPH-001.
VALID; DRAFT and dependency-gated by the Worker foundation.
VALID; DRAFT and dependency-gated by command roundtrip.
VALID; DRAFT and dependency-gated by read-model delta.
VALID; DRAFT and dependency-gated by read-model delta.
VALID; DRAFT and dependency-gated by both time controls and save-load-restart.
VALID; DRAFT and dependency-gated by hermetic E2E.
Listed RR1 parent as REVIEW and exactly seven RR1 child tasks as DRAFT.
No output; no RR1 implementation task is currently READY.

Only successor-Lead handoff delivery paths remained unstaged; they were preserved and excluded from the systems commit.

decisions.decision: Use Task Graph -> Web Worker Runtime -> Command Roundtrip -> Read Model Delta -> parallel Time Controls and Save/Load/Restart -> Hermetic E2E -> Exit Validation.
Keep all seven children DRAFT. Only the Worker foundation may become dependency-ready after this graph is accepted, integrated, and CLOSED on origin/main.

Assign non-overlapping allowed_paths across child tasks. The Worker foundation owns protocol and runtime seams; downstream tasks must request an explicit routed scope change rather than overlap shared ownership.
This commit contains process task contracts and handoff evidence only. It does not authorize or implement R1 product behavior.

Final validation is based on complete successor-Lead delivery commit 24a93d9cc536b3563afce0a0344438308eeb7962. Operational Lead thread 019f4d4f-3856-7d63-8b33-e1bf1b9be9ca owns terminal QA delivery, acceptance, and closure.

risks.summary: The R0 audit remains accurate until the child tasks are implemented: normal Web/Electron paths still lack authoritative Worker command/query/save/load/time-control wiring.
Strictly disjoint scopes require the Worker and command tasks to establish stable extension seams. Any later need to reopen a shared integration file must be routed and reviewed before writing.
Save/load/restart crosses Electron preload and filesystem boundaries, so security_reviewer, systems_architect, and qa_reviewer are all required.

requested_action: Independently validate the eight RR1 tasks, dependency gating, owner/reviewer/risk contracts, disjoint allowed paths, production-runtime acceptance coverage, JSON/handoff validity, and process-only scope. Return ACCEPT to Lead or exact REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK evidence; do not start R1 implementation before graph closure on origin/main.
